description:
A sustainability specialist at a logistics company wants to install a 100 kW rooftop PV array at a warehouse in Phoenix, AZ. The warehouse has a small daytime baseload but a massive nighttime load when electric delivery vans plug in to charge. Because the utility pays only wholesale rates for exported solar but charges high retail rates for nighttime imports, you need a custom hourly script to calculate the true annual utility bill savings. 

Write a Python script using PySAM that models a 100 kW DC commercial PV system using the Pvwattsv8 module (Fixed Roof Mount array_type=1, Standard module module_type=0, 14.08% losses) with a Phoenix TMY weather file. The script must generate an 8760-element hourly load array representing a 10 kW continuous load during the day (06:00 to 17:59 inclusive) and a 50 kW continuous load at night (18:00 to 05:59 inclusive). Perform an hourly energy balance for the year where grid imports cost $0.15/kWh and exports credit $0.04/kWh. Save the annual metrics (pv_annual_production_kwh, load_annual_demand_kwh, annual_grid_import_kwh, annual_grid_export_kwh, cost_without_pv_usd, cost_with_pv_usd, and annual_savings_usd) to a JSON file at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/ev_fleet_pv_analysis.json and save the script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/ev_fleet_analysis.py.
